event_blocks.summary: The video opens with a top-down view of Miss Fortune, a female champion in a pink and blue outfit with a cowboy hat and large gun, positioned in the bottom lane near the blue team's turret, actively farming minions. A male player/streamer introduces the segment, clarifying his intention to play full AP Miss Fortune in the mid lane, despite the initial bottom lane visual, noting it's "autofill action" and that his "mid lane days were quite a long time ago." The game clock starts at 0:00 with a 'Minions have spawned' notification, and the player begins moving toward the lane, opening Miss Fortune's rune page interface, displaying a standard AP build with 'Sorcery' and 'Precision' runes, stating the plan is to hit 1000 AP. As blue and red minions engage in combat, accompanied by their attack sounds, the player continues to farm, gaining gold and experience, with damage numbers appearing above enemies and the player reacting with sounds like "Bang! Exactly" to successful hits, though he admits to missing a minion. UI Elements display the player’s stats, including 30 attack damage, 23 armor, and 23 magic resistance, while the minimap in the bottom right shows champion positions, and social media overlays for Twitch and Twitter appear intermittently. The player explains his AP build, highlighting that his E has a 120% AP ratio and her ulti has a 25% AP ratio, and expresses surprise when an ability doesn't bounce as expected. Miss Fortune levels up twice, marked by 'LEVEL UP!' notifications, and her health and mana bars fluctuate with combat. At 0:11, she secures 'FIRST BLOOD!' on an enemy champion, triggering an announcement from a female game announcer, while the player exclaims "Oh!" and admits to "trolling," noting that almost all of Miss Fortune's damage abilities scale off AP. The Rune Page Interface briefly appears at 0:50, confirming the AP build, just as Miss Fortune herself is heard saying "Don't get cocky," before the player decides to recall, strategizing about the enemy's push. The bottom lane environment, with its stone pathways, grassy terrain, and glowing torches, remains consistent under stable lighting, focusing on Miss Fortune’s early-game dominance, underscored by subtle game music and constant in-game sound effects.

Rune Page Interface.description: A menu overlay showing Miss Fortune's rune setup, including the 'Sorcery' and 'Precision' paths, with various runes selected for an AP build, briefly appearing during the segment.

raw_segments: The video opens with a top-down view of a League of Legends game, showing the character Miss Fortune, a female champion in a pink and blue outfit with a cowboy hat and large gun, positioned near the blue team's base in the bottom lane. A male speaker immediately introduces the segment, stating, "Hey, welcome everybody to a new video. Today we'll be playing full AP Miss Fortune in the mid lane, switching it up a little bit from the jungle," clarifying his intended role despite the initial bottom lane visual. The environment is a rocky, grassy area with glowing torches and a stone turret to the left. As the game clock starts at 0:00, a 'Minions have spawned' notification appears, and the player begins moving toward the lane. The speaker continues, "Here's the rune page on screen and the plan this game, of course, is to hit a 1000 AP," as the player opens Miss Fortune's rune page interface, displaying a standard AP build with 'Sorcery' and 'Precision' runes. After closing the menu, the player proceeds to the lane where blue and red minions engage in combat, accompanied by the sounds of their attacks and abilities. The speaker further explains his mid lane role, noting it's "autofill action" and that he "used to be a mid laner before I was a jungler," later adding, "I maybe forgot to mention that my mid lane days were quite a long time ago," and referencing a missed last hit, "as you can tell by how I missed that minion." Miss Fortune uses auto-attacks and abilities to last-hit minions, gaining gold and experience, with damage numbers appearing above enemies and the speaker reacting with sounds like "Bang! Exactly" to successful hits. The UI displays the player’s stats, including 30 attack damage, 23 armor, and 23 magic resistance. The minimap in the bottom right shows champion positions, and social media overlays for Twitch and Twitter appear intermittently. The player continues to farm, maintaining position near the turret, as the game time reaches 1:00 with the score still 0-0, all set against a backdrop of subtle game music and constant in-game sound effects, creating a calm and strategic atmosphere focused on early-game farming and positioning.

The video opens with Miss Fortune, a female champion in a pink and blue outfit with a cowboy hat and large gun, positioned in the bottom lane near the blue team's turret, actively farming minions. A male streamer's voice immediately questions, "Why AP on an ADC, dude, are you trolling?" before explaining his build, stating, "My E is 120% AP ratio," and later, "her ulti is a 25% AP ratio," highlighting the strategic choice. Miss Fortune engages in combat with both Blue and Red Minions, using auto-attacks and abilities, accompanied by distinct gun-firing and ability activation sounds, to clear waves efficiently. The streamer comments on the farming, "We're actually farming pretty good," though he quickly retracts, "Oh, never mind," after an initial misjudgment. He expresses surprise when an ability doesn't bounce as expected, "What? How did that not bounce to her? That's crazy." As Miss Fortune levels up twice, a clear 'LEVEL UP!' notification appears visually and audibly, and her health and mana bars fluctuate with combat. The streamer continues to analyze his gameplay, discussing the priority of his 'Q' ability and feeling "scammed here" when it doesn't target champions. He considers putting another point into his 'E' ability, believing "that could actually really pay off 'cause I already have AP now." He anticipates a bounce, "That'll bounce the cannon surely, right?" and reacts with relief, "Yes, okay! I was like, no, the one time it actually bounces to her instead." The Minimap in the bottom right corner shows champion positions, and UI Elements at the bottom display stats. Text overlays promoting 'twitter @nightb3' and 'twitch.tv/nightb3' appear intermittently. The scene remains dynamic with continuous minion combat and Miss Fortune’s strategic positioning, underscored by the ongoing sounds of battle and the streamer's focused commentary on early-game lane control and resource management, including a brief mention of "Potion up here."

Miss Fortune efficiently clears waves of both Blue and Red Minions using auto-attacks and abilities, accompanied by the sounds of combat and the turret firing. The player continues to explain that almost all of Miss Fortune's damage abilities scale off AP. Throughout, her health and mana bars fluctuate, and the Minimap in the bottom right corner tracks her position and nearby enemy movements. The female game announcer is heard multiple times stating "An ally has been slain." At 0:50, the Rune Page Interface briefly appears, revealing her 'Sorcery' and 'Precision' rune paths, confirming the AP build, just as Miss Fortune herself is heard saying "Don't get cocky." The player then decides to recall, strategizing about the enemy's push. UI Elements dynamically update with her actions, including level, gold, and ability cooldowns, while Social Media Overlay Text promoting 'twitter @nightb3', 'twitch.tv/nightb3', and a Discord link appears intermittently. The bottom lane environment, with its stone pathways, grassy terrain, and glowing torches, remains consistent under stable lighting, focusing on Miss Fortune’s early-game dominance.

The combat intensifies with ability sounds as Miss Fortune uses her abilities, including Double Up and Bullet Time, supported by the Turret. Meanwhile, the team successfully slays the HexTech Drake in the river, as shown by the on-screen notification. Throughout, the Minimap tracks her movements, and Social Media Overlay Text promotes the streamer’s platforms, while the environment shifts from the base to the bottom lane and river, with dynamic lighting effects from abilities and combat.

event_blocks.summary: Miss Fortune engaged in combat near the river against enemy minions and a Turret, with the player commenting on item choices and exclaiming victory before respawning at base. Upon respawning, the player accessed the Shop Interface, purchasing items including Horizon Focus and noting the acquisition of Gathering Storm, later referencing significant damage numbers with Horizon Focus and Liandry's. Miss Fortune then returned to the bottom lane to farm Blue Minions, leveling up twice as indicated by UI Elements, with the player expressing excitement over damage numbers. A Rift Herald Notification appeared, signaling the enemy team's summoning of the Rift Herald, prompting the player to question the AP gain for its cost. Miss Fortune continued farming before engaging Lucian near a Turret, with the player urging an end to the game, then realizing the enemy team was not ending and deciding to intervene. Despite the player's attempt to clutch the situation, Miss Fortune was killed again, and the enemy team destroyed the Turret. The player resigned, attributing the lost opportunity to Sion, as the enemy team secured the HexTech Drake, granting them a buff. The enemy team then pushed further into the base, destroying the Inhibitor and Nexus, resulting in a Victory screen. Throughout these events, the Minimap displayed entity positions, Social Media Overlay Text promoted the streamer's channels, and UI Elements continuously updated game statistics.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Miss Fortune, a female champion in a pink and blue outfit with a cowboy hat and large gun, engaging in combat near the river, fighting enemy minions and the Turret. A male speaker, likely the player, comments, "I think boots are better though. Maybe we can win," then exclaims "We won!" with a laugh, adding, "It doesn't matter. You know what, if we won... then I need to get my AP back." He also jokes, "For the thumbnail!" After being killed, Miss Fortune respawns at the base, and the player immediately opens the Shop Interface, purchasing items including the Legendary item Horizon Focus. The speaker notes, "Oh, I got Gathering Storm too, perfect," and later excitedly references "Horizon Focus, 7,000" and "10,000 with Liandry's" in relation to damage numbers. She then returns to the bottom lane to farm Blue Minions, leveling up twice as indicated by the UI Elements, with the player exclaiming, "1091, let's go!" and "We did it!" while laughing. During this time, the Rift Herald Notification appears, alerting that the enemy team has summoned the Rift Herald. The player questions, "Why does this give so much AP for such a little money? That's actually insane," and mentions "1500, boom bam boom bang. Numbers." Miss Fortune continues farming and then engages Lucian, an enemy champion in a red and gold outfit wielding dual pistols, in a skirmish near the Turret. The player urgently calls out, "Guys, end, end, end," but then realizes, "Wait, they're not ending. Oh my god, they're not ending. I'm gonna have to come in, clutch. I'm coming in clutch." Despite her efforts, she is killed again, and the enemy team pushes the lane, destroying the Turret. The player then resigns, "Never mind. Sion, you stole my thunder." The enemy team secures the HexTech Drake, triggering a notification and granting them a team-wide buff. The battle intensifies as the enemy team pushes further into the base, destroying the Inhibitor and eventually the Nexus, leading to a Victory screen for the enemy team. Throughout the segment, the Minimap shows the positions of allies and enemies, and the Social Media Overlay Text promotes the streamer’s Twitch and Twitter handles, along with a Discord link, while the UI Elements continuously update with gold, level, and ability cooldowns, reflecting the dynamic state of the game.

event_blocks.summary: The video begins with a cinematic, stylized artwork of a lone figure in a red cloak in an autumnal forest, accompanied by a speaker's remark, "you should try it guys, it's really fun." This transitions to a League of Legends post-game statistics screen highlighting Teemo's 9/10/25 KDA and 20,292 gold, with the speaker noting "Damage dealt, 60,000 damage," acknowledging Ezreal's superior performance, and reflecting on not having played "MF in a while" or "mid lane." The focus then shifts to Teemo's rune page, detailing a Sorcery path with First Strike, Magical Footwear, Cosmic Insight, Absolute Focus, and Gathering Storm, with the speaker commenting on "2000 gold gained almost, 2400 on First Strike," and describing it as a "pretty fun rune page, especially if you're trying to hit 1000 AP on MF." As social media overlays appear, the speaker thanks viewers, encourages subscriptions for daily content, asks for suggestions on future videos, and bids farewell. The event concludes with a dynamic, animated dance sequence on a grid-like stage with glowing lights, featuring a futuristic golden and purple character performing to upbeat, electronic, rhythmic music with vocal samples and sound effects, ending with a large 'NB' logo.

Dance Stage.description: A futuristic, high-tech stage with a grid of glowing white circular lights, serving as the backdrop for the animated dance sequence. The stage's modern aesthetic is complemented by the driving, upbeat electronic music that plays during this outro segment.

Teemo.description: A small, green, mushroom-like champion with a red hat and a dart launcher, shown in the post-game stats screen with a 9/10/25 KDA, 212 kills, and 20,292 gold, indicating a high-impact performance in the match. The speaker references the game's outcome, noting "Damage dealt, 60,000 damage" and acknowledging that "Ezreal did outdo us here," while also mentioning not having played "MF in a while" or "mid lane."

Dance Character.description: A stylized, animated character in a futuristic golden and purple outfit, performing a dynamic dance on a grid-like stage with glowing white circular lights, culminating in the display of a large 'NB' logo, likely representing the content creator's brand. This sequence is accompanied by upbeat, electronic, rhythmic music featuring vocal samples and sound effects, creating a high-energy outro.

Social Media Overlay Text.description: Text overlays promoting social media handles such as 'twitter @nightb3', 'twitch.tv/nightb3', and a Discord link, appearing intermittently at the bottom left of the screen. These visuals accompany the speaker's concluding remarks, where he thanks viewers, encourages them to "subscribe to the channel to see more," mentions "we're posting daily now," and asks for comments on "what you guys want to see next."

Cinematic Artwork Scene.description: A visually rich, painterly scene depicting a cloaked figure in a mystical autumn forest with red and orange foliage, mist, and a soft, glowing sky, serving as an artistic transition or intro.

Rune Page Interface.description: A menu overlay showing Teemo's rune setup under the 'Sorcery' path, including runes such as First Strike, Magical Footwear, Cosmic Insight, Absolute Focus, and Gathering Storm, with detailed stats like damage dealt and bonus AD/AP. The speaker highlights its effectiveness, noting "2400 on First Strike" and calling it a "pretty fun rune page, especially if you're trying to hit 1000 AP on MF."

Post-Game Stats Screen.description: A detailed in-game statistics interface showing damage metrics, KDA, gold, and item builds, with a focus on Teemo's performance, including total damage to champions (60,787), total damage dealt (70,990), and damage taken (57,631). The speaker directly references these stats, stating "Damage dealt, 60,000 damage" and commenting on the game, including "Ezreal did outdo us here" and his experience playing "MF" or "mid lane."
